title: Inside the Encoder Block
concept: The Transformer encoder block contains two main components: a multi-head self-attention layer followed by a feed-forward neural network.

exercises.prompt: How many main components make up a standard Transformer encoder block?
exercises.explain: The concept explains that the encoder block contains two main components: self-attention and a feed-forward network.

cards.title: Peeking Inside the Machine
cards.body: Have you ever wondered how AI understands the exact meaning of a word based on its surroundings? It all happens inside a special structure called the Encoder Block! Let's peek inside to see how it works.

cards.title: Inside the Encoder Block
cards.body: The Transformer encoder block is a processing unit that updates the meaning of words. It does this using two main parts in order: first, a multi-head self-attention layer to find connections between words, and second, a feed-forward neural network to process each word further.

cards.title: The Two-Step Translation team
cards.body: Think of the encoder block like a two-step translation assistant. Step 1 (Self-Attention) is like looking at a whole sentence and highlighting how words connect to each other. Step 2 (Feed-Forward) is like looking at each highlighted word individually to polish its final meaning.

cards.title: Context in Action
cards.body: In the sentence "The bank of the river," the self-attention layer connects "bank" to "river" so the AI knows we aren't talking about money. Then, the feed-forward network updates the representation of "bank" to mean "sloped land near water."

cards.title: More than Attention
cards.body: Some people think that the encoder block only uses self-attention to process language. In reality, self-attention only helps words "talk" to each other. The feed-forward network is absolutely necessary to process and refine those words individually afterward!

cards.title: The Encoder Pipeline
cards.body: Let's look at how data travels and changes inside the encoder block step-by-step.

left.text: Words look at each other to gather context and build relationships.

right.text: Each word is processed individually to update its final meaning.

exercises.prompt: What are the two main components inside a Transformer encoder block?

options.text: A multi-head self-attention layer and a feed-forward network
A speech recognition engine and an image filter
A search engine and a database connector
Only a feed-forward network without any self-attention

exercises.explain: An encoder block is made of two main parts: a multi-head self-attention layer followed by a feed-forward neural network.

exercises.prompt: True or False: The feed-forward neural network comes after the self-attention layer in the encoder block.
exercises.explain: Yes, the self-attention layer is followed by the feed-forward neural network inside the block.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing component of the encoder block.
exercises.sentence: Inside an encoder block, the multi-head self-attention layer is followed by a [blank] neural network.
exercises.explain: The first layer (self-attention) connects the words together, and the second layer (feed-forward network) processes them further.

exercises.prompt: Which of these AI designs describes a correct Transformer encoder block?

options.text: "We use self-attention to let the words share context, and then a feed-forward network to update each word's meaning."
"We skip self-attention completely and only use a feed-forward network to read the words one by one."
"We just repeat the self-attention layer ten times without any other types of networks."

exercises.explain: An encoder block must contain both self-attention (to link words) and a feed-forward network (to process them). Option A correctly describes this combination.

exercises.prompt: How does the Transformer encoder block process input data?

options.text: It first runs a feed-forward network, then self-attention, and finally discards the connections.
It first connects words using multi-head self-attention, then refines them using a feed-forward network.
It processes words completely independently in both steps without ever linking them together.
It only uses self-attention and leaves out the feed-forward network entirely.

exercises.explain: The encoder block does not process everything in a single step. It first uses multi-head self-attention to link words together, and then passes the result through a feed-forward network to process each word's meaning individually.
